name: slack_list_channels
description: List Slack channels the bot has access to. Use when the user asks about available channels or wants to know where to post.

max_results.description: Maximum number of channels to return (default 20, max 100)

name: slack_read_messages
description: Read recent messages from a Slack channel. Use when the user asks what's been discussed in a channel, wants to catch up, or needs context from a specific channel.

channel_id.description: The Slack channel ID (e.g. C123ABC456). Use slack_list_channels first if you don't know the ID.

max_results.description: Maximum number of messages to return (default 15, max 50)

name: slack_search
description: Search messages across all Slack channels. Supports Slack search operators like from:user, in:channel, has:link, before:date, after:date. Use when the user asks to find a specific conversation, topic, or message.

query.description: Search query. Supports Slack operators: from:username, in:channel, has:link, before:2026-03-01, after:2026-01-01

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default 10, max 50)

sort.description: Sort by: score (relevance, default) or timestamp

name: slack_send_message
description: Send a message to a Slack channel. IMPORTANT: Always confirm with the user before sending. Show them the channel, message content, and ask for approval first.

channel_id.description: The Slack channel ID to post to. Use slack_list_channels first if needed.

text.description: The message text to send. Supports Slack markdown formatting.

thread_ts.description: Optional: reply in a thread by providing the parent message timestamp

name: slack_send_dm
description: Send a direct message to a Slack user. IMPORTANT: Always confirm with the user before sending. Show them the recipient, message content, and ask for approval first.

user_id.description: The Slack user ID to DM. Use slack_list_users first if you don't know the ID.

name: slack_list_users
description: List users in the Slack workspace. Use when the user asks about team members, wants to find someone's Slack ID, or needs to look up who's in the workspace.

max_results.description: Maximum number of users to return (default 20, max 100)
